assemblyTopic: problem 848 | convergence assembly | Explain the 282 First-Failure Mechanism
sourceOrpCommand: orp mode breakdown granular-breakdown --topic "problem 848 | convergence assembly | Explain the 282 First-Failure Mechanism" --json

sourceOrpResult.interpretation: The ORP command returned the mode scaffold, so this packet supplies the concrete assembly answers from the repo-owned p151 blocker, p479 repair handoff, frontier ledger, and p4217 guard.

parentAction.completionRule: A convergence assembly packet names the exact next non-ladder move: bulk selector cover, impossibility theorem, structural complement decomposition, ranked ledger transition, live-family binding, or an explicitly ledgered p479 availability split.

provedOrCertified: The p151 local/free exact-certification route is ledgered as a blocker/open leaf.
No p151 repair handoff or p151 availability split is allowed without a future exact squarefree certificate.
The only already recorded reserve inside this finite q97 screen was p479.
assembledPieces.boundary: This does not prove p151 impossible in principle; it only closes the local/free p151 route for this turn.

provedOrCertified: The p479 representative cross product is exactly factored squarefree.
p479 repairs the selected p443/q97 representative after p151 is ledgered.
assembledPieces.boundary: This does not prove p479 availability or squarefree hitting over the full q97 child.

provedOrCertified: open_frontier_obligation_count is 34 after the finite leaf handoff.
The growth tripwire is active after p151/p479 resolution.
The guard forbids another fresh selector child before a bulk, structural, ranked, or explicitly ledgered handoff is recorded.
assembledPieces.boundary: The frontier ledger is a local pressure measure, not a global proof of termination.

provedOrCertified: The mod-50 bounded CRT finite replay theorem is promoted and no longer needs another finite replay step before p4217 assembly.
All-future mod-50 recurrence and finite Q partition remain open, but they are not the immediate p4217 guard action.
assembledPieces.boundary: This does not close the p4217 complement.

supportedClaim.statement: The p443/q97 finite repair screen is consumed at representative level: p151 is a blocker/open leaf and p479 is exact-certified for the selected representative. The next allowed p4217 move must be an explicitly ledgered p479 availability split for the same q97 child, or a broader bulk/structural theorem. No fresh fallback-selector ladder is permitted.
doesNotClaim: p479 is available or squarefree-hitting on the full q97 child.

finiteMeasureOrNoMeasureYet.interpretation: The frontier expanded while descending. That is allowed only if the next step records a finite token, terminal leaf, bulk cover, impossibility theorem, structural decomposition, or ranked transition.

selectedFiniteToken.family: selected p443/q97 square-obstruction child inside the p61/q101 branch
selectedFiniteToken.rankUse: Partition the current q97 child by the already exact-certified p479 reserve endpoint before any fresh selector is introduced.

selectedHandoff.whyThisInsteadOfAnotherSibling: p479 is not a new fallback selector; it is the already recorded reserve inside the finite q97 repair screen and has an exact representative repair. Splitting by p479 availability consumes the finite p479^2 availability token for the same child instead of starting a naked p-next/q-next ladder.
selectedHandoff.wholeFamilyCovered: The selected p443/q97 square-obstruction child, not the whole p4217 complement.
selectedHandoff.finiteDenominatorOrPartition: Residues modulo 479^2 = 229441 for the p479 endpoint availability formula on the q97 child.
selectedHandoff.ifStepFailsBoundary: Emit a deterministic p479 availability blocker naming the missing formula or source parameter; do not introduce a new selector.
selectedHandoff.ifStepSucceedsBoundary: Emit a p479 availability split packet with available residues, unavailable residues, first available family, and each created child ledgered as available-child, unavailable-complement, terminal, or open.
selectedHandoff.whyCheaperThanBulkThisTurn: Bulk p4217 cover still spans 17781949 p4217-complement residues plus p61/p443/q97 descendants; the p479 split is the smallest guard-approved finite partition already supported by an exact representative repair.

rejectedShortcuts: Do not exact-certify a new p151 route without new factorization evidence.
Do not try p47, p53, p59, or any fresh fallback selector one by one.
Do not treat the p479 representative repair as q97 child coverage.
Do not report frontier growth as convergence without terminal leaves, bulk cover, structural decomposition, or ranked ledger accounting.

remainingPuzzleBoundary.smallestFalsifiableBoundary: Derive the p479 endpoint availability formula on the selected q97 child and split residues modulo 229441, or emit a blocker proving that formula/source is not available locally.
outsideThisAssembly: q97-avoiding part of the first p443-available family
q101-avoiding part of the p61 branch

oneNextAction.action: Derive and emit the p479 availability split for the selected p443/q97 child with denominator 479^2, or emit a deterministic blocker naming the missing formula/source parameter.
oneNextAction.command: node --check src/runtime/theorem-loop.js && node --check src/runtime/problem-progress.js && node --test test/p848-endpoint-menu-compiler.test.js test/p848-282-alignment-obstruction-packet.test.js
oneNextAction.completionRule: A p479 availability split packet partitions the q97 child into ledgered available/unavailable residues, or a blocker records why the p479 availability formula cannot be derived locally.

verificationCommand: node --check src/runtime/theorem-loop.js && node --check src/runtime/problem-progress.js && node --test test/p848-endpoint-menu-compiler.test.js test/p848-282-alignment-obstruction-packet.test.js
proofBoundary: This assembly chooses the next guard-approved handoff. It does not perform the p479 availability split, close the q97 child, close the p443-unavailable complement, cover the wider p4217 complement, or decide Problem 848.
nextTheoremMove: Derive the p479 endpoint availability split on the selected p443/q97 child using the finite 479^2 token, or emit a deterministic formula/source blocker without introducing any fresh selector.
